data.narrative:
Neros, a Series B defense and security company founded by Soren Monroe-Anderson and Olaf Hichwa, is evaluated against a comp universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage. The company has raised $121M to date.

Two valuation methodologies triangulate Neros's enterprise value. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $230M. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables—Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B)—produces a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $300M to $3.0B. The latter method reflects significant dispersion within the Defense & Security cohort at Series B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $195M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $615M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression models suggest downside around $230M, while peer comparables support substantially higher valuations depending on Neros's positioning relative to Dream Security and Harmattan AI versus lower-valued peers like Castelion. Key unknowns affecting placement within this range include revenue traction, customer concentration, and competitive differentiation relative to the peer set.
